frameworks.name: preparatory state / population dynamics
frameworks.one_line: pre-stimulus population state configures rule-appropriate sensory processing

frameworks.name: rule-based sensorimotor transformation
frameworks.one_line: context rule gates mapping from identical stimuli to motor responses

core_question: How does activity along a cortical sensorimotor stream encode the current rule and use it to shape rule-dependent responses to identical sensory stimuli?
key_assumptions: rule information must be represented in neural activity before and during stimulus
preparatory population states can causally shape subsequent stimulus-evoked responses
S1, S2, MM, ALM form a relevant cortical sensorimotor processing stream
manipulations: rule blocks: lick-to-tactile (reject visual) vs lick-to-visual (reject tactile)
recording site across the stream: S1, S2, MM, ALM
key_findings: single-unit activity in S1, S2, MM and ALM distinguished between the two rules both prior to and in response to the tactile stimulus
rule-dependent activity present on a single-trial basis across the stream
interpretations: rule-dependent preparatory population states are configured ahead of stimulus and may shape stimulus-evoked responses according to the current rule
